title: quercus lanuginosa var. croatica
intro: Quercus lanuginosa var. croatica, also known as the Croatian or hairy-leaved oak, is a variety of the Quercus lanuginosa tree, native to the Dinaric Alps region in Eastern Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which are attributed to its high tannin content. The medicinal properties of Quercus lanuginosa var. croatica have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and acting as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or mixing the leaves with a solvent, and are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
